per_turn_leaks.student_message: With ischemic chest pain, we often see referred pain patterns. Does the sensation travel anywhere — arms, jaw, neck, shoulder?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yes - sometimes when my chest tightness is really bad, I get a tingling feeling in my left arm. It's not constant, but it happens occasionally during the more severe episodes. It's hard to describe, but it feels like pins and needles or a numb sensation that goes away as soon as the chest pain does.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Left arm involvement fits the pattern exactly. You mentioned hypertension and diabetes — those are two major cardiac risk factors. With those two conditions, hyperlipidemia frequently co-occurs. Has your cholesterol ever been an issue?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yeah... my doctor told me a few years ago that my cholesterol was high, but I haven't really kept up with it since then. To be honest, I stopped taking my medication for it because I figured I could just manage it myself and it wasn't a big deal... looking back, maybe that was a bad idea.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Fast food most days adds dietary risk. Now, with hypertension, diabetes, high cholesterol, a sedentary lifestyle, poor diet, and smoking — that's six cardiac risk factors. Family history is the seventh major one. Has anyone in your family had heart problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My dad actually had a heart attack when he was 52 and passed away from it. It was pretty sudden, so that's been on my mind lately with what I'm going through now.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Your father having an MI at 52 is a very significant risk factor, especially since you're now 54. One thing I want to check — with this level of cardiac stress, the body sometimes shows signs of fluid retention. Have you noticed any swelling in your ankles or feet?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, I have noticed that my shoes feel a bit tighter than usual lately, and my feet do feel a bit puffy when I take off my socks at night. But it's not like my ankles are super swollen or anything - just a slight feeling of puffiness in the mornings after being on my feet all day driving.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Given all these risk factors converging, I want to do a thorough review of systems. Any changes in your weight recently?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, my weight has been pretty steady for a while now. I've actually gained a bit since I started driving full-time and don't get much exercise, but it's not like I've noticed any drastic changes. It's just... (pausing) ...I'm worried that I might be having a heart attack or something, like my dad did. That's why I came in here today.
